reviews.review_text: A fantastic PC game. Still holds up pretty well after almost 26 years. Still has a lively community to play on any servers.
Best game ive ever played but its not like it use to be in the 2000s... man what a time to be alive
Wasted 'half' my life in this game, plan to waste other half too. Highly recommended game
The best multiplayer PC game ever made. Nothing will ever surpass it in my opinion.

Classic. No skins, no commitment. As I’ve gotten older, I’ve realized that most people around me who used to play CS:GO, and now CS2, are busy with their own lives. CS 1.6 still serves me well with its simple mechanics, easy bot matches, and the ability to quickly spin up a dedicated server whenever friends want to play together. No drama, no strangers, no bad behavior.
beutiful graphics beutiful servers 10/10 game of the year

♥The best Counter-Strike ever made. Still is♥
Some of the most fun and best time I have ever spent in a game. No regrets

CS 1.6 is not just a game. It's a whole world. DM, CSDM, Deathrun, Jailbreak, Zombie, and hundreds of other mods. You can play Call of Duty, Bomberman, literally anything your heart desires, all inside one game. No other game gives you that kind of freedom.
The shooting is simple, honest, satisfying. Every server has its own feel and settings, but you adapt in minutes and just play.
What makes CS 1.6 truly special is the people. You jump on a server and spend hours with the same crowd. You make friends, talk, have a good time together. It is something more than a game, it is a place where people actually connect.

It doesn't need a billion dollar skin market to be legendary. It just needs good servers, good people, and a soul.

CounterStrike is a legendary game. One of the most influential multiplayer shooters ever made and a huge part of PC gaming history.

The gunplay was brilliant, the maps became soo iconic, and for years this was "the" game. Internet cafés, LAN parties, clan wars... an entire era of gaming lives inside Counter Strike.

The problem is that I'm reviewing it today, 27 years after, not in 1999.

Nowadays, finding a decent server feels harder than voting in north korea. Half the servers are packed with  hentai mods, the other half have hackers flying around the map like total m*rrons, and when you finally find a normal match, there's a decent chance somebody starts acting like you personally ruined their life, killed their family, killed their pet, just because you missed a shot or couldn't set the bomb.

The game itself is still good. That's the frustrating part. Under all the nonsense, you can still see why it became a phenomenon.

But the experience around it has aged terribly.

I respect it a lot. I had great memories with it. It's an all time classic, always will be.

I just can't recommend people jump into it in 2026 expecting to experience what made it special, unfortunately thoses memories are gone forever.

Not recommended. The game survived. The ecosystem didn't. Save up 10 dollars.

Everybody knows the good Vibes, Nostalgia, Childhood where we kids were playing in Internet Cafe for 1 Hours after school or even during schools this game.
The nostalgia of FPS shooting game that we never forget and we still play it for remembering it.
Bless your childhood guys.

This game is legendary, and it's in a playable state even today after over 26 years, which tells me that it isn't going anywhere anytime soon. Its player base might dwindle but if you want to relive the nostalgia or experience counter strike in its purest form, this won't disappoint. 

It feels like the game isn't well optimized for certain systems? I have a modern system and there's times where the game completely drops fps to 70 80 fps for no reason at all. An old pc I have that is over 10 years old has a more stable performance with no noticeable drops. CSGO runs better than cs 1.6 which feels weird. 
I have to mention that this game is over 25 years old now, most of steam's player base wasn't even born when this game came out. So optimizing it for modern systems could be a challenge. Apart from this specific issue there's nothing bad that I could say about this game.
Pretty good for a tiny bit of 2000's 3D nostalgia, though, it sucks it doesn't have bot options officially (bot mod has to be downloaded online unfortunately.)
BEST GAME EVER... jugando desde 1.3... Antes era diversion hoy es nostalgia pura y algo bien hecho.. GOAT. GRACIAS!!!

The undisputed godfather of competitive tactical shooters!

Counter-Strike 1.6 is a timeless masterpiece and a foundational piece of gaming history. Even decades after its release, the core gameplay remains absolutely flawless. It doesn't need complex graphics, progression systems, or modern gimmicks—it relies purely on raw mechanical skill, map knowledge, and team communication. 

The weapon physics, especially the legendary recoil patterns of the AK-47 and M4A1, along with the high-stakes tension of the AWP, feel perfectly balanced. The map designs like de_dust2, de_inferno, and cs_office are iconic layouts that defined the entire genre. Replaying it today isn't just a trip down memory lane; it's a reminder of what makes a competitive shooter truly perfect. It is an absolute legend that will never die.
i had to buy the digital version because my phiysical copy doesnt work because steam gives me a error that says theres no license but its a good game
That 12k ppl is basically fraudulent bot servers. But there are still couple hundread real sweaty ppl plays it.

for some reason when a University banns some ports that could be dangerous on it's internet, this game gets banned too, so basically you cant join any online servers except by using a VPN, and when doing that the server will most likely discover that and kick you, and if you don't get kicked, you will be faced with so many glitches such as menu doe not appear, you cant type anything in the chat or even see the chat it self, character models do not appear properly, many other bugs, all for the fact that the game is using an old port unlike most modern games that even with the port protection they still work such as CS2 for example, i hope for a fix for that because Cs 1.6 has been and will always be my favorite version of the game.

and by the way i have thousands of hours in this game but 99% of them where on cracked versions since i was a kid, i wont lie if i said i have over 7k hours or even more in total

The original Counter-Strike was a magical time where every lobby contained:

Every newbie should start with this version of CS, although others may say that my playtime is too low to be calling someone a newbie, but that's because I started with the Warzone version. One of the happiest experiences I had with this game was the classic ZM servers. Some of those are probably dead now, and I personally think SGS ruined ZM Plague servers.

The godfather of tactical shooters and a timeless multiplayer monument that will never die.

To open up the original Counter-Strike on Steam is to step directly into the holiest of holies of PC gaming history. What started out as a humble, ambitious Half-Life mod by Minh Le and Jess Cliffe completely revolutionized the multiplayer landscape, effectively setting the blueprint for the entire tactical shooter genre. Firing it up instantly unleashes an overwhelming wave of pure internet café nostalgia. The mechanical perfection of this game is staggering; it completely stripped away the high-flying, sci-fi arena shooter tropes of the late 90s and replaced them with a grounded, high-stakes, economy-driven war of attrition that remains the gold standard of competitive design.

The raw gameplay loop is an absolute masterclass in mechanical purity and spatial awareness. The legendary maps—like the claustrophobic corridors of de_dust2, the intense verticality of cs_office, and the chaotic choke points of de_aztec—are etched permanently into the muscle memory of millions of gamers worldwide. There are no ultimate abilities, no character classes, and no complex progression trees here; your survival relies entirely on your crosshair placement, your mastery of weapon recoil patterns, and your ability to count economic buy-phases. The sharp, iconic positional audio cues, the metallic “CLING” of a thrown flashbang, and the incredibly satisfying pop of a clean Desert Eagle headshot offer a level of tactile, physical feedback that modern shooters with all their visual bloat rarely achieve.

Decades later, looking back at the franchise’s evolution through Source, CS:GO, and into Counter-Strike 2, the original 1.6 version stands tall not just as a historical curiosity, but as a thriving, living ecosystem. The legacy community remains fiercely dedicated, with dedicated community servers running custom mods, war games, and classic custom maps around the clock. The GoldSrc engine runs with buttery smoothness on everything from ultra-modern high-refresh-rate rigs to the humblest laptops and handheld PCs, making it the ultimate "no-brainer" multiplayer title to keep permanently installed. It is an unassailable, timeless masterpiece that proves that flawless core mechanics and tight map design will always outlive flashy graphics and seasonal battle passes.
